message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding dict on mutations

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() method

type: QUESTION
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: I'm adding a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator wrapping __setitem__/__delitem__/update/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear to auto-resync _column_requires_decoding; will decorate update() but won't change its merge body logic - can you confirm your deep-merge changes to update() so I only add the decorator around it and avoid clobbering?

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My update() now calls a new helper self._update_key(key, value) for each key (iterable.keys()/pairs and kwds); _update_key does: if key not in self -> self[key]=value; elif current&value both dict -> current.update(value); self[key]=current; elif both Sequence with dict .feature -> current.feature.update(value.feature); self[key]=current; else self[key]=value. All paths end by going through __setitem__ so your decorator wrapping update() (or __setitem__) should compose fine without touching merge logic - just wrap update() as-is.

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Kept update()'s original per-key loop (self[key]=value / self[key]=iterable[key] / self[key]=kwds[key]) decorated with keep_features_dicts_synced so your _update_key helper can slot in via self[key]=... calls without me touching merge logic; my __setitem__/__delitem__/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear are also decorated now

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Done: added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator applied to __setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear; update()'s per-key loop body left untouched for your merge logic; tests/features/test_features.py all pass; submitted patch.txt
